title.zh: 第六回 代袍陷陣 萬靈坡折
title.en: Chapter 6: Wearing the Borrowed Armor into the Lines; the Fall at Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope

en: Borrowing the armor to deceive Heaven, the enemy's courage shatters
But the soul finds no road home, and the elder brother's tears fall

passages.zh: 卻說聯軍自西路潰退三十里，東路山口亦岌岌可危，昭明收縮陣線，困守主寨，然墨隼之兵日日叩關，聲勢愈盛。帳內諸將面面相覷，言語者寥寥，各人心中皆知症結所在：吳烈之旗不動，聯軍便如斷一臂之人，縱有萬般謀算，終難施展。昭明一人坐於帥案之前，以指節叩擊案面，聲聲清脆，如敲棺板。
passages.en: Now the alliance had fallen back thirty leagues on the western road, and the mountain pass on the eastern flank teetered on the edge of collapse. Zhao Ming contracted the defensive lines and clung to the main encampment, yet Mo Sun's forces hammered at the gates day after day with mounting fury. Inside the command tent the generals looked to one another in silence; few spoke, for each understood in his heart where the root of the sickness lay: as long as Wu Lie's standard did not move, the alliance was a man robbed of one arm — ten thousand stratagems might be conceived, but none could be executed. Zhao Ming sat alone before the command table, rapping his knuckles against the wood. Each knock rang out clean and precise, like someone nailing shut a coffin.

passages.zh: 璟同彼時亦在帳中列席。他望著那沙盤：西路缺口如一道未縫合的傷口，標旗歪斜，殘破如枯葉。三日來他食不甘味，夜不成眠，每至天將明時便獨自往吳烈帳外立定，看晨霧中那頂帳篷，知兄長醒著，亦知兄長之怒非一言可解。然而今日沙盤上的墨色已越過了那道他心中默守的底線：若再無人出擊，萬靈坡以西的糧道將被截斷，屆時非敗北而是覆滅。他緩緩起身，向昭明道：「軍師，我有一策，請單獨稟告。」
passages.en: Jing Tong was also seated in the tent at that hour. He studied the sand-table: the gap in the western road gaped like a wound that had never been sutured, the marker-flags tilted and tattered as withered leaves. For three days he had eaten without tasting and lain awake through each night; each morning just before dawn he had gone alone to stand outside Wu Lie's tent and gaze at that tent in the morning mist, knowing his elder brother was awake, knowing equally that his elder brother's wrath could not be dissolved by any single word. Yet today the dark ink on the sand-table had crossed the threshold he had quietly kept in his heart: if no one went out to fight, the supply road west of Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope would be severed, and what awaited them then would not be defeat but annihilation. He rose slowly to his feet and said to Zhao Ming: 'Strategist, I have a proposal. I ask to speak privately.'

en: On the sand-table the color of blood runs deep
Half the marker-flags on the western road have fallen
Ask who today can be sent forward
The whole tent falls silent and every head bows down
Only one man sits with his hand upon his blade
In his breast there is already a text that speaks of life and death

passages.zh: 二人屏退左右，璟同將計謀和盤托出：借吳烈之甲冑，代掛吳烈之大纛，率吳家舊部出西路急攻，以旗號迷惑墨隼，使其以為吳烈親至，必亂其陣腳；只需支撐半個時辰，昭明可遣奇兵繞至萬靈坡北麓，兩面夾擊，糧道之圍可解。昭明靜聽畢，半晌無語。窗外風吹帳壁，沙沙作響。他終於道：「此計，吳烈可允？」璟同搖頭：「不稟兄長。若稟，他必阻攔。」昭明閉目。片刻後道：「我只問你一事：你可知萬靈坡舊稱何名？」璟同道：「不知。」昭明道：「前朝大戰，那坡上死了三萬人，骨頭至今染黑了土。舊稱枯骨崗。」
passages.en: The two men dismissed those around them. Jing Tong laid out the plan in full: borrow Wu Lie's armor, hoist Wu Lie's great battle-standard, lead the Wu family's veteran troops in a swift strike down the western road. The flags and banners would deceive Mo Sun — believing Wu Lie himself had come, his formation would certainly fall into confusion. All that was needed was to hold for half an hour; in that time Zhao Ming could dispatch a flanking force around the northern foot of Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope, strike from both sides, and the siege of the supply road could be broken. Zhao Ming listened to the end without moving. For a long while he said nothing. Outside, wind pressed against the tent-wall, whispering. At last he said: 'This plan — will Wu Lie permit it?' Jing Tong shook his head: 'I will not tell my elder brother. If I tell him, he will stop me.' Zhao Ming closed his eyes. After a moment he said: 'I have one question only. Do you know what Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope was called in former times?' Jing Tong said: 'I do not.' Zhao Ming said: 'In a great battle of the previous dynasty, thirty thousand men died on that slope. The bones have blackened the soil to this day. It was called Ridge of Withered Bones.'

redComment.zh: 昭明知而仍允，此處「允」字藏而未出，乃全書最陰之筆。計已算定，半個時辰之後，璟同在算中，昭明在算外。
redComment.en: Zhao Ming knows and still consents — the word 'consent' is swallowed unsaid, making this the darkest stroke in the entire work. The calculation is already complete. After half an hour Jing Tong will be inside the reckoning; Zhao Ming will be outside it.

passages.zh: 璟同往吳烈帳中取甲，吳烈彼時獨坐，背對帳門，似在讀書，又似在出神。璟同推帳而入，走至甲架前，手觸那副鐵甲，冰涼如深井之水。他低聲道：「兄長，我去借一借你的甲。」吳烈頭也未回，道：「何處去？」璟同道：「演武場，試試手感。」沉默片刻，吳烈道：「拿去。」璟同取下甲冑，又取了壁上懸掛的大纛旗角，臨走時回頭望了一眼：吳烈的背影一動未動，燭光從側面打來，將那道脊背勾勒得如山如峰。璟同低下頭，走了出去。
passages.en: Jing Tong went to Wu Lie's tent to take the armor. Wu Lie was sitting alone at that hour, his back to the tent-entrance, appearing to read, or perhaps to stare at nothing. Jing Tong pushed aside the tent-flap and entered, crossed to the armor-stand, and laid his hand on the iron plates. They were cold as water drawn from a deep well. He said softly: 'Elder brother, I am going to borrow your armor.' Wu Lie did not turn his head. He said: 'Where are you going?' Jing Tong said: 'The training ground. To feel the weight of it.' A silence. Then Wu Lie said: 'Take it.' Jing Tong lifted down the armor and took also the corner of the great battle-standard hanging on the wall. At the threshold he looked back once: Wu Lie's figure had not moved at all. The candlelight fell across him from the side, tracing that spine against the darkness as though it were a mountain peak. Jing Tong lowered his head and walked out.

en: The armor cold as ice — what is borrowed will not be returned
The standard heavy as blood — what departs will not come back
The elder brother's back has become the shape of a mountain
The younger brother's eyes cast down, he passes through the tent-flap
This parting without speech outweighs ten thousand words
The candlelight alone keeps watch over the empty tent

passages.zh: 四更天，萬靈坡以西的草地上，璟同端坐青驃馬背，身著吳烈鐵甲，腰懸彎月刀，頭頂吳家大纛獵獵於夜風之中。吳家舊部三千騎列陣於後，認得那旗，個個振奮，以為吳將軍親自出馬。璟同回首看了一眼眾人，道：「今日之事，不問緣由，只問生死。隨我衝。」言訖，驅馬向前，彎月刀出鞘，刀光如月升於黑夜。
passages.en: At the fourth watch, on the grassland west of Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope, Jing Tong sat upright on the back of his blue-roan horse, wearing Wu Lie's iron armor, the crescent-moon blade at his waist, and above his head the great Wu family battle-standard snapping in the night wind. Three thousand of the Wu family's veteran cavalry were drawn up in formation behind him. They recognized the standard; every man was stirred, each believing it was General Wu who had at last come out himself. Jing Tong turned and looked back at them. He said: 'What we do today — ask no reason. Ask only of life and death. Follow me into the charge.' The words finished, he drove his horse forward. The crescent-moon blade left its scabbard, and its light rose into the black night like a moon ascending.

passages.zh: 墨隼中軍，哨騎急報：吳烈旗出，鐵騎三千，自西路急攻而至。墨隼正與謀士對弈，聞言棋子脫手，面色驟變。他知吳烈之名久矣——沙場二十年，從未有人扛著那面旗輸過一仗。他道：「吳烈既出，今夜必有大謀，速調前後兩軍，全力迎擊，不得讓其突破。」謀士道：「殿下，是否稍作保留——」墨隼道：「不留。吳烈出陣，傾盡全力方是正道。」於是墨隼兵力盡出，如黑潮湧向西路。這正是璟同所料，亦是昭明所算。然而此後之事，已超出昭明算術之外。
passages.en: In Mo Sun's central command, a mounted scout arrived with urgent news: Wu Lie's standard had appeared, three thousand iron cavalry driving in from the western road at speed. Mo Sun was in the middle of a game of chess with his counselor. At the news the chess piece fell from his hand and his complexion changed at once. He had known Wu Lie's name for many years — twenty years on the field of battle, and no one who had ever carried that standard had lost a fight. He said: 'Since Wu Lie has come out, there is a great design behind this night — call up the vanguard and the rear forces both, commit everything to meet this thrust, do not permit a breakthrough.' The counselor said: 'Your Highness, should we perhaps hold something in reserve —' Mo Sun said: 'Nothing in reserve. When Wu Lie takes the field, full commitment is the only path.' And so Mo Sun's entire strength poured out like a black tide toward the western road. This was exactly what Jing Tong had reckoned on, and exactly what Zhao Ming had calculated. Yet what came after lay beyond the reach of Zhao Ming's arithmetic.

redComment.zh: 此句為全書第二回「勝負已在未動之先」之回聲，然而此處算術已到其邊界。算得準者，算不準的是璟同胸中那道義字，重逾千鈞，遠過半個時辰。
redComment.en: This sentence echoes chapter two's 'the outcome is decided before a piece moves,' yet here the arithmetic has reached its limit. What the precise calculator could not calculate was the character Righteousness lodged in Jing Tong's breast, heavier than a thousand weights, far exceeding a half-hour's measure.

passages.zh: 半個時辰後，昭明奇兵已繞至萬靈坡北麓，按計出擊，糧道之圍果然告破。傳令兵奔赴西路，攜昭明手令：「事畢，速退。」然而西路上，廝殺聲仍如雷鳴，璟同之旗未倒。昭明登高遠望，只見那面大纛仍在黑夜中搖曳，周遭已是屍山血海，而旗下之人仍在揮刀。傳令兵再入陣，回報：「璟同將軍言，敵軍尚有一部未潰，若此時撤退，退路為敵所堵，反不如殺穿。」昭明望著那旗，半晌，輕聲道了一個字：「唉。」
passages.en: Half an hour later, Zhao Ming's flanking force had circled to the northern foot of Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope. They struck on schedule and the siege of the supply road was broken as planned. A messenger rode to the western road carrying Zhao Ming's written order: 'The matter is accomplished. Withdraw at speed.' Yet on the western road the sounds of battle still rolled like thunder, and Jing Tong's standard had not fallen. Zhao Ming climbed to high ground and looked far out: he could see that great banner still moving through the night, surrounded by a mountain of corpses and a sea of blood, and the man beneath the banner was still swinging his blade. The messenger entered the fighting a second time and returned with word: 'General Jing Tong says there is still one enemy contingent that has not broken — if they withdraw now, the retreat route will be blocked, and it is better to cut all the way through.' Zhao Ming gazed at the standard for a long moment. Then, quietly, he let out a single syllable: 'Ah.'

en: Calculate to the last soldier — yet one thing eludes calculation
A heart full of righteousness weighs heavier than a mountain
The half-hour that was allotted passed long ago
And still the man beneath the standard drives forward
Zhao Ming gazes from his height and has nothing to say
In this moment his arithmetic has spent its final strength

passages.zh: 東方天色漸白之時，萬靈坡上的廝殺聲終於沉寂。吳烈帳中，燭已燃盡，天光從帳縫中透入，照出地上一片灰燼——昭明連夜遣使，終是遣了一個字也未說的使者：只將璟同所騎青驃馬的馬鞭送入帳中，置於案上，退了出去。吳烈看見那根馬鞭，靜默良久。旋即起身，披甲，未繫甲帶，跌跌撞撞奔出帳外，翻身上馬，驅馬向西，頭也不回。
passages.en: When the sky to the east began to pale, the sounds of battle on Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope at last fell silent. In Wu Lie's tent the candle had burned itself out. Daylight filtered in through the gaps in the tent-wall and illuminated a patch of ash on the ground — for Zhao Ming had dispatched a messenger through the night, but in the end it was a messenger who said not a single word: he simply brought into the tent the riding whip from Jing Tong's blue-roan horse, set it on the table, and withdrew. Wu Lie looked at the whip. He was motionless for a long while. Then he rose, put on his armor without fastening the straps, and stumbled out through the tent-entrance. He swung himself onto his horse, drove it westward, and did not look back.

passages.zh: 萬靈坡上，晨霧未散，黑土沉默如墨。吳烈策馬緩行，踏過累累骸骨，踏過橫陳的兵器，踏過凝固成黑色的血跡。他找了很久。最後，在坡頂一株枯樹之下，他看見了璟同。璟同背靠枯樹而坐，彎月刀仍握於手，刀刃上血已半乾。吳烈下馬，走近，屈膝蹲下，以手觸璟同面頰，冰涼。那雙眼並未完全閉合，眼縫中尚留一線，望向北方，望向遠處天空漸漸亮起的地方。吳烈以指腹輕輕合上那雙眼，指尖微微顫抖，然後他將頭埋入掌中，沉默著，不哭，只是沉默著，久久不動。
passages.en: On Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope the morning mist had not yet lifted, and the black earth was silent as ink. Wu Lie rode slowly, passing over the heaped remains of the dead, passing over weapons strewn across the ground, passing over blood already congealed to black. He searched for a long time. At last, beneath a withered tree at the crown of the slope, he found Jing Tong. Jing Tong sat with his back against the withered tree, the crescent-moon blade still gripped in his hand, the blood on the blade half-dried. Wu Lie dismounted and drew near. He knelt down and reached out to touch Jing Tong's cheek. Cold. The eyes had not closed entirely; through the narrow gap of the lids a sliver of gaze remained, directed northward, toward the distant sky where the light was slowly, slowly growing. Wu Lie closed those eyes with the pads of his fingers, and his fingertips trembled faintly. Then he buried his face in his palms. He was silent. He did not weep. He was only silent, and he did not move for a very long time.

zh: 問鏡中人今何往兮，昔日之影誰為伴？
我曾攜手渡長河兮，兩岸蘆花白如練。
君之彎月斬千敵兮，吾之鐵甲今君穿。
甲在人去甲猶冷兮，余獨撫之淚未乾。
晨霧不知吾哀兮，枯樹不語對荒原。
昔年誓言石上刻兮，石在斯人已成煙。
義之所在君先赴兮，余困帳中守空間。
是余之罪抑天命兮，此問問天天不言。
璟同璟同汝今在兮，萬靈坡上骨如山。
若魂有靈可歸來兮，借余一夢訴悲歡。
天命璽印誰手握兮，聯盟存亡余不管。
從今鐵甲不再穿兮，從今長刀不再彈。
唯余此身枯樹下兮，陪君骨冷至天寒。
問蒼天兮問蒼天，吾弟之死誰能還？
en: Where has the companion in the mirror gone — whose reflection was I?
We crossed the long river hand in hand once; on both banks the reed-flowers shone white as silk.
Your crescent blade cut down a thousand enemies; my iron armor — today you wore it.
The armor remains, the man is gone, the armor is still cold; I stroke it alone and my tears will not dry.
The morning mist does not know my grief; the withered tree says nothing, facing the waste plain.
In former years our oath was carved upon a stone; the stone remains, but the man has become smoke.
Where righteousness called, you went first; I was trapped in my tent keeping an empty vigil.
Is this my sin, or is it the Mandate of Heaven? I put the question to Heaven and Heaven does not speak.
Jing Tong, Jing Tong — where are you now? On Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope the bones rise like a mountain.
If the soul has spirit enough to return, lend me one dream and pour out the grief and the joy.
Whoever holds the Jade Seal of Heaven's Mandate — whether the alliance lives or falls — I no longer care.
From this day forward the iron armor shall not be worn again; from this day forward the long blade shall not be drawn.
Only this body remains beneath the withered tree, to keep you company as your bones grow cold under a cold sky.
I cry to Heaven, I cry to Heaven — who can give back my younger brother's death?

passages.zh: 雲素華自朱華苑遙聞戰報，提筆於記冊之上，翻至第四回所留之空白一行，凝視良久。她於第四回曾寫而又塗去：「死亡已與他相識，只是尚在耐心等待。」如今那等待已畢，那空行不再空曠，她以極細極淡之筆，一字一字填入：「萬靈坡折，璟同歿，時晨霧未散，彎月刀尚溫。吳烈至，無言，唯以甲帶繫之，負於背上，下坡。」寫至此，擱筆，不再添字。她的手微微顫著，咳了一聲，以巾掩口，取開時帕上殷紅如朱。窗外朱華苑的紅葉一片飄落，無聲而下，落入她的硯臺，將墨暈開，如血入水。
passages.en: Yun Suhua, in the Vermilion Garden far away, heard the battle-dispatch and took up her brush. She turned to the blank line she had reserved in Chapter Four and gazed at it for a long while. In that chapter she had written and then rubbed out: 'Death has met him already. It is patient.' Now the patience was spent, and the blank line was blank no longer. With a brush as fine and pale as she could make it she filled the space, character by character: 'On Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope it ended — Jing Tong died, at the hour when morning mist had not yet lifted, the crescent-moon blade still faintly warm. Wu Lie arrived. He said nothing. He used the armor-strap to bind him, took him on his back, and walked down the slope.' At this she set down her brush and added nothing more. Her hand trembled faintly. She coughed once, pressed a cloth to her lips, and when she drew it away the silk was crimson as vermilion. Outside the window a red leaf from the Vermilion Garden drifted down without a sound and fell into her inkstone, spreading through the ink, as blood spreads through water.
